records.ety: [F. sacre (cf. It. sagro, Sp. & Pg. sacre), either fr. L. sacer sacred, holy, as a translation of Gr. "ie`rax falcon, from "iero`s holy, or more probably from Ar. çaqr hawk.]

defs: A falcon (Falco sacer) native of Southern Europe and Asia, closely resembling the lanner.
notes: ☞ The female is called chargh, and the male charghela, or sakeret.

quotes: On the bastions were planted culverins and sakers.
The culverins and sakers showing their deadly muzzles over the rampart.
